content: Document: Shipday Tutorial | Category: ONBOARDING > 3RD PARTY DELIVERY SETUP

BOTTLECAPPS
Shipday Tutorial
Shipday is a delivery management software platform designed to help small businesses (like
alcohol retailers) manage their local delivery operations efficiently. It provides tools to streamline
the process of receiving, dispatching, and tracking delivery orders, whether using a business’s
own drivers or third-party delivery services.

own drivers or third-party delivery services.
In this guide, we will demonstrate how it works with your Bottlecapps app and/or website.
Note: You likely already have a Shipday account, but if not, you will need to create one before
getting started.
First, login (Login Screen):
After logging in, click “Integrations” in the upper right corner:

[Screenshot Description]: 1) The admin screen displayed is the Shipday login screen.

2) The visible elements include fields for entering an email address and password, a "Forgot Password?" link, and a "Sign In" button. There is also a "New to Shipday? Sign Up" option.
3) The elements highlighted or pointed to include the email address and password fields, the "Sign In" button, and the "Integrations" link in the upper right corner (mentioned later in the tutorial).

4) The user is being instructed to log in to their Shipday account by entering their email and password and then clicking the "Sign In" button. After logging in, they are advised to click "Integrations" in the upper right corner.

From this page, click “API Credentials” on the left and then click “Show API Key” as shown
below. Then COPY that key and email it to help@bottlecapps.com and provide your exact store
name:
On that same page, scroll down and you will see “Webhook Setup.” Click “Add API Link” and
input the following link here:
Webhook; https://liquorapps.com/Bcapi/api/BCGateway/ShipDayOrderUpdate

API Key: qwertyuio:poiuytrewq
Once setup, here’s how Shipday will work. When a DELIVERY order comes in, open the order
in your Webconsole (in the orders tab) by clicking the blue Order Number:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot displays the following information:

1. **Admin Screen/Section**: It is the "Integrations" section of the Bottlecapps admin interface, specifically focusing on API Credentials and Webhook Setup.

2. **Visible Elements**:
   - Buttons: "Show API Key" and "Add API Link."
   - Fields: API Key input area, Webhook URL input area.
   - Menus: The left navigation menu listing options like "Dispatch," "Orders," "Drivers," "Map," "Reviews," "Reports," and "Integrations."

3. **Highlighted Elements**:
   - The "API Credentials" option is circled in green.
   - The "Show API Key" button is pointed to with an arrow.
   - The Webhook URL link and the input with the example API Key are highlighted.

4. **User Action Instructions**: The user is instructed to click on "API Credentials," then "Show API Key," copy the key, and email it to help@bottleapps.com. Additionally, the user is guided to scroll down to the Webhook Setup section, click "Add API Link," and input the provided webhook URL and API Key.

Then, scroll down and lick “Update Status to Scheduled for Delivery.” This will send the order
over to Shipday:
Next, open your Shipday Dashboard and click Dispatch. Find the order you just scheduled for
delivery and you can then assign the driver:
NOTE: Before assigning drivers, drivers must be added. To do this, click the phone icon in the
upper right:

upper right:
Drivers can scan the QR code or you can send them the link:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot displays the following information:

1) **Admin Screen/Section**: The screen shows the "Shipday Dashboard" which is part of the Bottlecapps admin interface for managing deliveries.

2) **Buttons, Fields, Menus, or Settings**: 
   - A prominent button labeled **“Update Status to Scheduled for Delivery”**.
   - Other buttons include **“Cancel Order”** and **“Print Order”**.
   - The **Dispatch** section is visible along with a listing of **New Orders**.
   - There is also an **Assign** button associated with individual orders alongside driver details.

3) **Highlighted Elements**: 
   - An arrow points to the **“Update Status to Scheduled for Delivery”** button.
   - An arrow also points to the **“Dispatch”** option.
   - An additional arrow indicates the **“Assign”** button near a specific order.

4) **User Action Instructed**: The user is instructed to scroll down and click the **“Update Status to Scheduled for Delivery”** button to send the order to Shipday, followed by opening the Shipday Dashboard, clicking on **Dispatch**, and finding the scheduled order to assign a driver. Additionally, there’s a note about adding drivers through the phone icon before assigning them.

Then, select the driver:
From the Driver App, drivers can then accept or decline the delivery:

[Screenshot Description]: 1) The admin screen displayed is the "Shipday Tutorial" section focused on dispatching orders.

2) Visible elements include a pop-up window to download the Dispatch App with a QR code, options to "Copy link" and "Print QR code," and the section where drivers are listed with their status and assigned orders.

3) The QR code is prominently highlighted, and the driver "Anirudh R." is marked as "Online." The number of assigned orders is indicated as "3."

4) The user is being instructed to download the Dispatch App using the provided QR code and then select a driver from the list to assign an order.

The delivery driver will then be able to update the status of the order(s):

[Screenshot Description]: 1) The admin screen displayed is the "Waiting" section of the Shipday app.

2) The visible buttons include "Reject" (red) and "Accept" (green). The screen also shows information about the distance (3.05 mi), order number (W446196), recipient name (Abhishek odela), scheduled time (09:00 AM), and delivery address.

3) There are no specific highlighted elements, circles, or arrows in the screenshot.

4) The user is being instructed to accept or reject the order by clicking the respective buttons.

Once delivered, the delivery driver will get then be prompted to get proof of delivery via a photo
of the delivered item(s) and a signature:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot displays the "Orders" section of the Shipday admin interface.

1) **Admin Screen/Section**: The "Orders" screen is presented, showing a list of current delivery orders.

2) **Visible Elements**: 
   - There are several entries with order statuses such as "On the way," "Picked up," and "Started."
   - Each entry includes order numbers (e.g., #W446184), customer names, addresses, delivery times, and buttons labeled "Call," "Navigate," "Mark as Complete," and "Mark as On the way."

3) **Highlighted Elements**: 
   - The statuses ("On the way," "Picked up," "Started") appear to be highlighted for easy identification. 
   - Buttons like "Mark as Complete" and "Mark as On the way" are prominent.

4) **User Action Instruction**: Users are instructed to update the status of the deliveries by using the "Mark as Complete" or "Mark as On the way" buttons depending on the current status of the order. 

Overall, the screenshot guides users on how to manage and update order statuses within the Shipday application.
[Screenshot Description]: This screenshot displays the "Orders" section of the Shipday admin interface.

1. **Admin Screen/Section Displayed**: The Orders screen where active delivery orders are listed.
   
2. **Visible Buttons, Fields, Menus, or Settings**: 
   - "Mark as Complete" button for completing an order.
   - "Mark as On the way" button.
   - Menu icon.
   - Additional sections labeled "Map," "Waiting," "Messages," and "Performance."

3. **Highlighted Elements**: A pop-up dialog box is highlighted, featuring:
   - "Proof of Delivery (POD)" title.
   - "Take Proof of Delivery (POD)" button.
   - "Failed Delivery" button.
   - "Close" button.

4. **User Action Instruction**: The user is instructed to either take proof of delivery or mark the delivery as failed by selecting the corresponding button in the pop-up dialog.

NOTE: to enable Proof of Delivery as a requirement for your drivers (as well as other settings),
click settings, then Driver Settings:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot displays the "Proof of Delivery (POD)" admin section of the Shipday application.

1. **Admin Screen Section**: The section is labeled "Proof of Delivery (POD)".
  
2. **Visible Elements**:
   - Fields for inputting the name of the person who signed (labeled "Name of the person signed (Required)").
   - An area for entering additional notes (labeled "Enter Your Note").
   - Buttons for adding an image ("Add Image") and adding a signature ("Add Signature").
- A "Complete the Order" button at the bottom.

3. **Highlighted Elements**: No specific elements are highlighted, circled, or pointed to with arrows in the screenshot.

4. **User Instruction**: The user is instructed to enable "Proof of Delivery" as a requirement for drivers by clicking settings, then selecting "Driver Settings".

Meanwhile, all orders can be tracked from the Shipday Dashboard. It will show, for example,
when a delivery has started, been picked up, is on the way, or completed:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot displays the "Driver Settings" section of the Bottlecapps admin interface.

1) **Admin Screen or Section**: Driver settings within the Bottlecapps admin dashboard.

2) **Visible Buttons, Fields, Menus, or Settings**: 
   - Toggle settings for:
     - Show driver phone number to customers
     - Show sensitive customer details
     - Item check on pick-up
     - Require proof of delivery
     - Requires ID scanning
     - Option for drivers to re-optimize routes
   - A list of orders with statuses including "Picked Up" and "Started."
- A "Reassign" button next to each order.

3) **Highlighted or Pointed Elements**: 
   - "Picked Up" status is highlighted in green.
   - "Started" status is also highlighted in green.

4) **User Instruction**: The user is being instructed to track orders from the Shipday Dashboard, with a focus on the statuses of deliveries, such as when they have started or been picked up.

Once an order has been delivered, you will see that the order has been updated to DELIVERED
in your Webconsole:
INTERNAL USE ONLY:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot from the 'Shipday Tutorial' depicts the following:

1) **Admin Screen/Section**: The admin screen displayed is the "Dispatch" section for managing orders.

2) **Visible Buttons, Fields, Menus, or Settings**: There are order rows showing details such as order status ("On the Way"), order IDs (e.g., W446184), amounts ($71.02), and a "Reassign" button for each order. There are also navigation tabs for "Dispatch," "Orders," and "Drivers."

3) **Highlighted Elements**: The order status "On the Way" is highlighted in a green circle, and the status "Delivered" is highlighted in a subsequent section of the tutorial, pointing out the update in the Webconsole.

4) **User Action Instruction**: The user is instructed to recognize that once an order has been delivered, it will be marked as "DELIVERED" in the Webconsole.

Our team will select Shipday as your Delivery Provider and input the API Key you sent to
help@bottlecapps.com into the backend for your store:

[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot from the 'Shipday Tutorial' admin section displays the following:

1. **Admin Screen/Section**: The section shown is the "Delivery Providers" setup within the backend of the BottleCapps admin interface.

2. **Buttons, Fields, Menus, or Settings**: 
   - Radio buttons for selecting a delivery provider: options include "Own Delivery," "Delivery Solution," "DoorDash," "Ziffy," "FedEx," "DoorDash/FedEx," "Instacart," "Uber," and "Shipday."
   - A text input field labeled "API Key."

3. **Highlighted Elements**: The API Key input field is visibly emphasized, with the input obscured for privacy.
4. **Action Instruction**: The user is instructed to select "Shipday" as their Delivery Provider and input the API Key they sent to help@bottlecapps.com into the backend of their store.

BOTTLECAPPS
Renew Apple Developer Membership
Please follow the steps below to renew your Apple Developer Program membership.
Without this membership, your apps will not appear in the app store AND we will not be able
to publish an update to your app.
1. Visit developer.apple.com.
2. Click on "Account" at the top.
3. Sign in with your store's Apple ID email.
4. There will be a notification at the top that says "Renew Membership". Click and follow the
instructions to pay the $99 fee to Apple.
5. Once completed, go back to the homepage (developer.apple.com) and select "Membership Details".

6. There will be an option for "Auto-Renew Membership". Make sure this box is ticked so that
your account is always renewed.
If you need additional help, schedule a 15 minute call here: Help with Renewing Apple Account
Document: Renewing Expired Godaddy Domain | Category: ONBOARDING > HELP CENTER

BOTTLECAPPS
Renewing Expired GoDaddy Domain
To renew your expired GoDaddy domain, follow these steps based on the information available
as of March 11, 2025:
1. Sign In to Your GoDaddy Account:
Go to the GoDaddy website and log in to your Domain Portfolio. If you’ve forgotten
your credentials, use the “Find your username or password” option to recover them.
2. Locate Your Expired Domain:
Once logged in, navigate to your Domain Portfolio.
If you don’t immediately see your expired domain, filter your list by selecting
“Expiration” above the domain list, then choose “Expired” to display only domains
that are past their expiration date.
3. Access Renewal Options:
Select the expired domain you want to renew to open its Domain Settings page.
Look for the “Renew Now” option on the Renew card and click it.
4. Choose Renewal Settings:
Select your desired renewal term (e.g., 1 year, 2 years, etc.).
If applicable, check the box next to “Turn on Auto-renew” to prevent future
expirations.

4. Choose Renewal Settings:
Select your desired renewal term (e.g., 1 year, 2 years, etc.).
If applicable, check the box next to “Turn on Auto-renew” to prevent future
expirations.
Click “Continue” to proceed.
5. Complete the Checkout Process:
Review your renewal settings and any additional fees (see below for details on
potential costs).
Finish the process by completing the checkout. Your domain should be active again
within 90 minutes, with its updated expiration date reflected in your account.
Important Notes on Timing and Fees:
Grace Period (Day 0 to Day 18): For the first 18 days after expiration (for .com domains),
you can renew at the standard renewal price without extra fees. GoDaddy will attempt
auto-renewal on the expiration date and again on day 12 if it’s enabled.
Redemption Period (Day 19 to Day 30): Starting on day 19, a redemption fee (typically
around $80, though this can vary) is added to the standard renewal cost. You can still
recover the domain during this window.

Auction Phase (Day 26 Onward): On day 26, the domain may enter a GoDaddy auction.
You can still attempt to renew it with the redemption fee until day 30, unless someone
places an active bid, at which point renewal becomes unavailable.
Beyond Day 30: If the domain reaches day 36, it enters a final closeout auction. After day
72, it’s removed from your account and released back to the registry, making renewal
impossible unless it becomes available again for registration.
Additional Tips:
Check the Timeline: If you don’t see your domain in the expired list, it may be past the
renewal or redemption period. Contact GoDaddy support to confirm its status.
Special Cases: Some domain extensions (e.g., ccTLDs) have unique expiration rules, so
the timeline might differ slightly.
Prevent Future Issues: Enable auto-renew and ensure your payment method is up to
date to avoid expiration hassles.
If your domain is already in the auction phase or beyond redemption, you may need to bid on it

Document: Bottlecapps Data Integration | Category: ONBOARDING > ONBOARDING TUTORIALS (App & Web)

BOTTLECAPPS
Bottlecapps Data Integration
Bottlecapps has a partnership with over 60 different point-of-sale systems that utilize a wide range of
software suites to assist retailers with their in-store sales. The integration with each of these systems can
be broadly categorized as follows:
1. On Premise Database
2. Cloud Based
3. Mapping Tool
4. Flat File
On Premise Database:
This type of point-of-sale system usually has a designated server that houses all the data related to the
products and various attributes associated with it. The most common database used for such systems is
SQL Server. Bottlecapps requires you to download a remote login software such as TeamViewer to
facilitate the integration with our webconsole.
Once TeamViewer is installed, please proceed to providing the TeamViewer ID and Password to your
Bottlecapps Onboarding Manager. Our Data Integrations team will then access the server remotely to

Once TeamViewer is installed, please proceed to providing the TeamViewer ID and Password to your
Bottlecapps Onboarding Manager. Our Data Integrations team will then access the server remotely to
install a “Mobile App Updater” that can be set to run as frequently as you would like. The recommended
frequency is once every hour although this can be changed to run every 15 minutes or 30 minutes if
needed.
The Mobile App Updater (MAU) connects to the database in the server and generates a file that is
mapped to the Bottlecapps file specifications. This file is in CSV format and contains all the product
attributes such as SKU, UPC, Product Name, Description, Retail Price, Sale Price, Tax rates, Categories
and so on. The file is then sent to a specific folder within our server and processed within 10 minutes of
the file being sent. This ensures accurate inventory information for your customers.
Cloud Based:

the file being sent. This ensures accurate inventory information for your customers.
Cloud Based:
This type of point-of-sale system is a more modern version of the On Premise Database system. A cloud-
based point-of-sale (POS) system enables businesses to process transactions and manage sales data
using internet-connected devices. It stores information on remote servers, providing real-time access to
sales data from anywhere, facilitating inventory management, analytics, and customer relationship
management. Bottlecapps can connect to many of these systems using an API (Application
Programming Interface) that many companies offer. This usually requires a set of authentication values
like a Client ID and Client Secret Key that should always be maintained securely to prevent any
unauthorized use.
By reaching out to the service provider and receiving the keys, Bottlecapps will proceed to generate a file
per the file standards and set up the data feed.

unauthorized use.
By reaching out to the service provider and receiving the keys, Bottlecapps will proceed to generate a file
per the file standards and set up the data feed.
An advantage of a cloud based point-of-sale system is the ability to allow order inserts. This prevents a
need to manually input any online orders received through the app or website.

Mapping Tool:
The Bottlecapps Mapping Tool is used for a select few point-of-sale systems that are cloud-based, yet do
not have an API (Application Programming Interface) that Bottlecapps can use to connect. If there is no
API that we can use to connect to the system but the software allows an export of the data within the
system, Bottlecapps can create a tool that converts the data within this exported report to the
Bottlecapps file specifications to update the inventory.
The Mapping Tool can work in 2 ways:
1. Bottlecapps requires remote access to the computer that will be used to generate the inventory reports.
A script will be deployed within this system that will transfer the report from a designated folder within the
system to the Bottlecapps server.
2. Bottlecapps can deploy the Mapping Tool in the server directly thereby reducing the need to login
remotely to the client’s computer. By logging in to the Bottlecapps webconsole, retailers will be able to

remotely to the client’s computer. By logging in to the Bottlecapps webconsole, retailers will be able to
directly upload the export to the server by following the steps below:
Login to liquorapps.com and go to the Imports tab.
Select Upload RAW File, browse for the report, select it and hit Upload
2.
The selected file will then be securely sent to the Bottlecapps server and processed within the next
15 minutes.
This process can be repeated as many times as needed to maintain accurate inventory information.
Flat File:
Bottlecapps webconsole has a wide range of features for stores that are not inventory controlled or do
not use a retail point-of-sale system. The retailer can choose to create their own curated list of products
to sell online, or use a flat inventory quantity for all the items they maintain on their own.
The inventory can be maintained by logging in to liquorapps.com -> Prices & Inventories tab:

Select the items to be edited by clicking the Edit option right next to the item, and modify all the content
to be edited. When done, hit the Update button and it will reflect the changes online immediately.

content:
BOTTLECAPPS
How to Get a DUNS Number
To get a DUNS number (Data Universal Numbering System number) for your business, you’ll
need to request it from Dun & Bradstreet (D&B), the organization that assigns and maintains
these unique nine-digit identifiers. Here’s a step-by-step guide to help you obtain one as of
March 11, 2025:
Steps to Get a DUNS Number:
1. Check if You Already Have One:
Before applying, use D&B’s free D-U-N-S Number Lookup tool (available on their
website) to see if your business already has a DUNS number. This can happen if a
supplier, lender, or partner has previously requested information about your company
from D&B.
2. Visit the Dun & Bradstreet Website:
Go to the official D&B site (dnb.com) and look for the “Get a D-U-N-S Number”
option, typically found under the DUNS Number section or in the top navigation.
3. Select Your Purpose:
You’ll be asked why you’re requesting a DUNS number (e.g., for business credit,

metadata.source: documents\ONBOARDING\ONBOARDING TUTORIALS (App & Web)\How to Get a DUNS Number.pdf
metadata.source_filename: How to Get a DUNS Number.pdf
metadata.document_title: How To Get A Duns Number

content:
option, typically found under the DUNS Number section or in the top navigation.
3. Select Your Purpose:
You’ll be asked why you’re requesting a DUNS number (e.g., for business credit,
government contracts, or partnerships like Apple/Google developer programs).
Choose the option that fits your needs. For general purposes, the free option is
sufficient.
4. Provide Business Information:
Fill out the online form with the following details:
Legal business name
Physical address (P.O. boxes may not be accepted in some regions)
Phone number
Business owner’s name or primary contact
Legal structure (e.g., LLC, corporation)
Year the business was established
Primary industry and number of employees (if applicable)
Ensure this matches your official records (e.g., IRS EIN or Secretary of State filings)
to avoid discrepancies.
5. Submit Your Request:

metadata.source: documents\ONBOARDING\ONBOARDING TUTORIALS (App & Web)\How to Get a DUNS Number.pdf
metadata.source_filename: How to Get a DUNS Number.pdf
metadata.document_title: How To Get A Duns Number

content:
After entering your details, submit the application. In some cases, D&B may request
additional verification documents (e.g., Articles of Incorporation, business
registration, or a utility bill) to confirm your business’s legitimacy.
6. Wait for Processing:
The standard processing time is up to 30 business days, and it’s free. You’ll receive
your DUNS number via email once approved.
If you need it faster (e.g., within 5-8 business days), D&B offers expedited options for
a fee (around $229-$299, depending on the region and service), but this isn’t
necessary for most users.
7. Verify and Use:
Once you receive your DUNS number, log in to your D&B account (create one if
needed) to review your business profile. Keep it updated for accuracy, as lenders
and partners may reference it.
Key Tips:
Free Option: The basic DUNS number is free in many regions (e.g., the U.S.), though
processing times vary. Avoid third-party services charging unnecessary fees.

Free Option: The basic DUNS number is free in many regions (e.g., the U.S.), though
processing times vary. Avoid third-party services charging unnecessary fees.
Regional Differences: If you’re outside the U.S., check your local D&B site (e.g.,
dnb.co.uk for the UK, dnb.co.in for India) as processes and costs might differ. Some
countries charge for registration or require additional steps.
Expedited Needs: For urgent cases (e.g., Apple Developer Program enrollment), note that
free expedited service may be available for specific purposes—check with D&B support.
No Renewal Required: Once assigned, your DUNS number doesn’t expire, but you
should update your business info annually with D&B to maintain its usefulness.

verify your company with partners like Apple, Google, or suppliers. It’s not mandatory to run a

verify your company with partners like Apple, Google, or suppliers. It’s not mandatory to run a

If you hit a snag (e.g., your region isn’t supported or you don’t get a response within the

Document: Teamviewer Setup | Category: ONBOARDING > ONBOARDING TUTORIALS (App & Web)

BOTTLECAPPS
TeamViewer Setup
Visit the TeamViewer Website:
Go to the official TeamViewer website by typing "teamviewer.com" into your web browser's address bar.
Navigate to the Free Download Page:
Once on the TeamViewer website, navigate to the "Download" section. You can find this at the top of the
page or by scrolling down to locate it.

Select Your Operating System:
TeamViewer is available for various operating systems including Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and
iOS. Click on the appropriate download link for your operating system.
Start the Download:
Click on the download link to start the download process. The download should begin automatically, but
you may be prompted to confirm the download depending on your browser settings.
Locate the Downloaded File:
Once the download is complete, locate the downloaded file. This is typically found in your browser's
download folder unless you specified a different location.
Install TeamViewer:
Double-click on the downloaded file to start the installation process. Follow the on-screen instructions to
install TeamViewer on your computer.
Accept License Agreement:

During the installation process, you will likely be presented with a license agreement. Read through the
agreement and if you agree to the terms, click on the "Accept" or "Agree" button to proceed with the
installation.
Choose Installation Options:
You may be given the option to choose certain installation settings such as the installation directory and
whether to create shortcuts. Make your selections and proceed with the installation.
Wait for Installation to Complete:
The installation process may take a few moments to complete. Once it's finished, you should see a
confirmation message indicating that TeamViewer has been successfully installed on your computer.
Launch TeamViewer:
After installation, you can launch TeamViewer by locating it in your computer's applications menu or by
double-clicking on the desktop shortcut if you chose to create one during installation.
Set Up TeamViewer (Optional):

double-clicking on the desktop shortcut if you chose to create one during installation.
Set Up TeamViewer (Optional):
Depending on your intended use, you may need to set up a TeamViewer account or configure certain
settings. Follow the on-screen prompts to complete the setup process.
Start Using TeamViewer:
With TeamViewer installed and set up, you can now start using it for remote access, online meetings, file
transfers, and more.

The first and most important step to secure unattended access is to disable the random password within
the TeamViewer settings. To disable the random password, click Settings (⚙) ➜ Advanced
settings ➜ Security ➜ Random password (for spontaneous access) ➜ Choose Disabled (no
random password) ➜ Click OK.

The first and most important step to secure unattended access is to disable the random password within
the TeamViewer settings. To disable the random password, click Settings (⚙) ➜ Advanced
settings ➜ Security ➜ Random password (for spontaneous access) ➜ Choose Disabled (no
random password) ➜ Click OK.

Under Select Payment Method choose Refund a Credit Card
Provide customer information as requested on the Virtual Terminal screen. Minimum information needed to issue a refund
includes the original transaction ID, the last four digits of the account that was charged, and the amount of the refund.

When issuing a refund for a transaction, the transaction ID assigned to the original transaction must be entered in the "Original
Transaction ID" field. The Transaction ID can be located by viewing the original transaction details.
In a transaction details, only the last four digits of the credit card number or bank account are viewable.
Click Submit.

BOTTLECAPPS
Authorize.net - Setup and FAQ
Q: How do I issue a refund using Authorize.Net?
A: To issue a refund using Authorize.Net, log in to your account and navigate to the Transactions tab. Find the transaction you want to
refund, click on it, and then click the Refund button. Enter the refund amount and any necessary information, then click Submit.

To issue a refund using the Virtual Terminal, follow these steps:
1. Log into the Merchant Interface.
2. Click Virtual Terminal from the left side menu.
3. Under Select Payment Method choose Refund a Credit Card
4. Provide customer information as requested on the Virtual Terminal screen. Minimum information needed to issue a refund

includes the original transaction ID, the last four digits of the account that was charged, and the amount of the refund.
5. When issuing a refund for a transaction, the transaction ID assigned to the original transaction must be entered in the "Original
Transaction ID" field. The Transaction ID can be located by viewing the original transaction details.

6. In a transaction details, only the last four digits of the credit card number or bank account are viewable.
7. Click Submit.

A: To test a transaction using Virtual Terminal, log in to your Authorize.Net account and go to Virtual Terminal. Enter the credit card
number (e.g., 4111111111111111), expiration date, and the 3 or 4 digit number for the CVV code. For the address, use a valid

What is the purpose of the API Login ID, Transaction Key, Signature Key and Public Key for Authorize.net, and how can I
obtain them?
The API Login ID and Transaction Key are unique to your account. They are used to connect your website or other integrated business

application to the Authorize.net Payment Gateway for transaction processing. Please note that they are not valid for logging
into the Merchant Interface. The API Login ID is sensitive account information and should only be shared on a need-to-know basis, for
example with your Web developer for the purposes of integration with the payment gateway.
API Login ID

API Login ID
The API Login ID is a complex value that identifies your account to the payment gateway when submitting transaction requests from
your website. The API Login ID is at least eight characters in length and includes uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and/or
symbols.
Transaction Key

symbols.
Transaction Key
The Transaction Key is a 16-character alphanumeric value that is randomly generated in the Merchant Interface and is used as an
additional layer of authentication when submitting transaction requests from your website. The Transaction Key will not be visible at

any other time in the Merchant Interface. You must record it temporarily or copy and paste it to a secure file location immediately. Like
the API Login ID, the Transaction Key is sensitive account information and should only be shared on a need-to-know basis, for example
with your Web developer for the purposes of integration with the payment gateway.

Steps to Generate Your API ID and Transaction Key
Sign into the Merchant Interface.
Select Account from the main toolbar.
Under Security Settings, select API Credentials & Keys.
Based on the need and integration, select New Transaction Key or New Signature Key.
To disable the old Transaction or Signature Key, check the box labeled Disable Old Transaction/Signature Key Immediately.

If the Disable Old Transaction/Signature Key check box is not selected, the old Transaction or Signature Key will automatically
expire in 24 hours.
Select Submit to continue.
Request and enter PIN for verification.
Your new Transaction/Signature Key is displayed.

Q: What are some common error codes when using Authorize.Net?
A: Some common error codes you may encounter when using Authorize.Net include:
E00027: This error indicates that the transaction was declined due to an invalid payment method.
E00039: This error occurs when the transaction is not allowed based on the current account settings.

E00040: This error indicates that the transaction was declined due to an issue with the card number.
E00041: This error occurs when the transaction is declined due to an issue with the card expiration date.
E00054: This error indicates that the transaction was declined due to an issue with the card CVV code.

If you encounter any of these errors, double-check the payment information you entered and ensure that your account settings are
configured correctly.
How to access and setup Advance Fraud Detection Suite?
Log into the Merchant Interface.
Click Fraud Detection Suite in the main left side menu.
Click Setup Wizard.
Follow every step in the Wizard, so your settings will be successfully applied.
[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot shows the "API Credentials & Keys" section of the Authorize.Net admin interface.

1) **Admin Screen/Section**: API Credentials & Keys.
   
2) **Visible Elements**: 
   - API Login ID
   - Transaction Key
   - A button labeled "Create New Keys" with options for "New Transaction Key" and "New Signature Key"
   - A "Submit" button
   - A "Cancel" button
   - Text advising on security measures regarding the API and Signature Keys

3) **Highlighted Elements**: An arrow points to the "API Login ID" field, indicating its importance.

4) **User Action Instruction**: The user is instructed to disable the old Transaction Key and/or Signature Key by checking the relevant option immediately or else it will automatically expire in 24 hours.

You can navigate through the wizard using Save (to save the changes) or Back (to go back to a previous setting) buttons located at the
bottom of the page. If you decide not to enable a certain filter or setting right away, simply click Skip›› at the bottom of the page. The

only time settings are saved is when you click the Save button (regardless of whether you clicked the check box to enable the filter). You
can click Exit at any time to leave the Wizard, or wait until the end to click Finish. Regardless of if you leave the Wizard early or not,
any settings you save will stay saved.
Advance Fraud Detection Suite Filters and Tools

Advance Fraud Detection Suite Filters and Tools
Card Testing Settings: These filters help protect your account from abuse by fraudsters who are testing credit cards, and let you identify
high-volume fraud attacks.
Daily Velocity Filter: This filter allows you to specify a threshold for the number of transactions allowed per day. All transactions

exceeding the threshold in that day will be flagged and processed according to the filter action.
Hourly Velocity Filter: This filter allows you to specify a threshold for the number of transactions allowed per hour. All transactions
exceeding the threshold will be flagged and processed according to the filter action.

Suspicious Transaction Filter: This filter triggers on highly suspicious transactions using proprietary criteria identified by
Authorize.net. All transactions that meet this criteria will be flagged and processed according to the filter action.
[Screenshot Description]: The screenshot displays the "Advanced Fraud Detection Suite" section within the Bottlecapps admin tutorial.

1. **Admin Screen Section:** The "Advanced Fraud Detection Suite" (AFDS) settings for transaction filtering and fraud detection.
   
2. **Visible Elements:** 
   - "Suspicious Transaction Reports" with "Authorized/Pending Review" and "Pending Review" indicators.

- Transaction filters: "Velocity Filter," "Suspicious Transaction Filter," "Shipping/Billing Mismatch Filter," and "Transaction IP Velocity Filter."
   - "IP Administration" section with "Tool Name" and "Configuration" options.
   - General settings headers: "Transaction Search," "Setup Wizard," "Customer Response," "Email Notification," and "Documentation."

3. **Highlighted Elements:** 
   - The "Advanced Fraud Detection Suite" section is prominently boxed in red at the top.
   - Specific filters listed under "Transaction Filters" are indicated by numbers and configuration status.

4. **User Action Instructions:** Users are guided to click on a filter to configure its settings or to view related tools and to utilize the "Save" button to apply changes. They can also choose to skip settings or exit the wizard at any time.

Transaction IP Velocity Filter: This filter allows you to specify the maximum number of transactions allowed from the same Internet
protocol (IP) address per hour. All transactions that meet this criteria will be flagged and processed according to the filter action.
Advanced Integration Method (AIM) transactions must include the customer IP address (x_customer_ip) and user of Authorize.net API

must include customerIP in order to use this tool.
Transaction Settings: These filters let you identify high risk transactions through Address Verification, Card Code Verification, and
setting a maximum order amount.
Enhanced AVS Handling Filter: The Address Verification Service (AVS) is a standard feature of the payment gateway that compares

the address submitted with an order to the address on file with the customer's credit card issuer. Merchants can then choose to reject or
allow transactions based on the AVS response codes. AFDS includes an AVS filter that assists the decision process by allowing
merchants the additional options of flagging AVS transactions for monitoring purposes, or holding them for manual review.

Enhanced CCV Handling Filter: Like AVS, Card Code Verification (CCV) is a standard feature of the payment gateway. CCV uses a
card's three- or four-digit number to validate customer information on file with the credit card association. Like the AVS Filter, the CCV

Filter allows merchants the additional options of flagging CCV transactions for monitoring purposes, or holding them for manual review.
Amount Filter: Set lower and upper transaction amount thresholds to restrict high-risk transactions often used to test the validity of

credit card numbers. Transactions that are submitted with an amount less than your lower limit or greater than your upper limit will be
flagged and processed according to the filter action selected. Be careful when setting an upper limit amount, as any combination of
higher priced items may be purchased. You may want to search your transaction history for the highest transaction amounts you've

processed in the past to determine an appropriate upper limit.
E-Commerce Settings: These filters help prevent common e-commerce fraud scenarios by verifying and comparing billing addresses,
shipping addresses, and IP address locations.
Shipping Address Verification Filter: Verifies that the shipping address received with an order is a valid postal address.

IP Shipping Address Mismatch Filter: Compare the shipping address provided with an order to the IP address of where the order
originated from. This helps to determine whether or not the order is shipping to the country from which it originated. Advanced
Integration Method (AIM) transactions must include the customer IP address (x_customer_ip) and user of Authorize.net API must

include customerIP in order to use this tool.
Regional IP Address Filter: Flag orders coming from specific regions or countries. You can choose to customize the filter actions based
on an entire geographic area, or select country by country how to process transactions flagged by the filter.

Shipping-Billing Mismatch Filter: Identify high-risk transactions with different shipping and billing addresses, potentially indicating
purchases made using a stolen credit card. If the two addresses do not match, the transaction is flagged and processed according to the

filter action selected. This filter does not consider extra white spaces (the space key) at the end of the address information and is not case
sensitive when checking for a mismatch. You may want to keep in mind that address mismatches are common with gift transactions and

do not always indicate suspicious transactions. In order to use this filter, you must require both shipping and billing address information
on your website's payment form; otherwise the filter cannot compare information.
IP Address Administration: These filters let you block specific customers’ IP addresses to prevent known sources of fraud. If you use

our APIs, you may also specify which server IP addresses should be trusted by the payment gateway.
Authorized API IP Addresses: Configure a list of authorized server IP addresses from which API transactions will be accepted by the
payment gateway. Any API transaction submitted from an IP address not included on this list will be rejected.

IP Address Blocking: IP Address Blocking allows you to block transactions submitted from IP addresses known to be the source of
suspicious or fraudulent transactions. Advanced Integration Method (AIM) transactions must include the customer IP address
(x_customer_ip) and user of Authorize.net API must include customerIP in order to use this tool.
Actions that can be performed on these filters

Process as normal and report filter(s) triggered: When this action is selected, transactions that trigger this filter are processed as
normal, but are also reported in the Merchant Interface as triggering this filter. This action is useful if you want to "test" your filter
settings.

settings.
Authorize and hold for review: When this action is selected, transactions that trigger this filter are sent for authorization, and upon
successful authorization are placed in the Authorized/Pending Review state. Once in Authorized/Pending Review, you will have 30 days

to manually review and either approve or void the transaction. If no action is taken in the 30-day period, the transaction will expire. This
action is useful if you want to review authorized transactions prior to submitting for settlement.
Do not authorize, but hold for review: When this action is selected, transactions that trigger this filter are placed in the Pending

Review state prior to being sent for authorization. Once in Pending Review, you will have 5 days to manually review and either approve
or decline the transaction. Once you approve the transaction, it is sent for authorization. If no action is taken in the 5-day period, the

transaction will expire. This action is useful if you want to review transactions prior to authorization to avoid incurring any associated
authorization fees.
Decline the transaction: When this action is selected, transactions that trigger the filter will be declined automatically prior to
authorization. This is the most severe action you can take for a transaction.

What is Address Verification Service (AVS) and how to use and configure it?
You can configure the AVS filter settings in the Merchant Interface to specify when a transaction should be rejected based on the results
of the AVS match. The following result codes are possible:

A = Street Address: Match - First 5 Digits of ZIP: No Match - This signifies a perfect match between the street address entered by
the customer and the billing address on file with the credit card issuing bank, but a mismatch between ZIP codes.
B = Address not provided for AVS check or street address match, postal code could not be verified - This signifies that the

transaction was submitted to Authorize.net without address information, so the AVS check could not be performed.
E = AVS Error - This signifies that an error occurred on the processing network while processing the AVS request, so AVS
information is not available for this transaction.

G = Non U.S. Card Issuing Bank - This means the credit card-issuing bank is not of U.S. origin and does not support the AVS
system.
N = Street Address: No Match - First 5 Digits of ZIP: No Match - Neither the street address nor the ZIP code provided by the
customer matches the billing address and ZIP code on file with the card issuing bank.

P = AVS not applicable for this transaction - This code is returned when address information is not checked against the AVS
system. Examples include eCheck transactions, credits, voids, prior auth capture transactions, capture only transactions, declines, and
other transactions that do not include address checking.

R = Retry, System Is Unavailable - This means AVS was unavailable on the processing network or the processor did not respond.
S = AVS Not Supported by Card Issuing Bank - This means the card issuing bank does not support AVS.

What is Card Code Verification (CVV) and how to use and configure it?
You can configure the CCV filter settings in the Merchant Interface to specify when a transaction should be rejected based on the results
of the CCV match. The following result codes are possible:

U = Address Information For This Cardholder Is Unavailable - This means address information is not available for the
customer's credit card at the processor.
W = Street Address: No Match - All 9 Digits of ZIP: Match - The nine-digit ZIP code provided matches the billing ZIP code on
file with the card-issuing bank, but the street address provided does not match.

X = Street Address: Match - All 9 Digits of ZIP: Match - The nine-digit ZIP code and street address provided matches the billing
ZIP code and street address on file with the card issuing bank.
Y = Street Address: Match - First 5 Digits of ZIP: Match - The five-digit ZIP code and street address provided matches the billing
ZIP code and street address on file with the card-issuing bank.

What is Card Code Verification (CVV) and how to use and configure it?
You can configure the CCV filter settings in the Merchant Interface to specify when a transaction should be rejected based on the results
of the CCV match. The following result codes are possible:
M = Match - The CVV2 code entered matched that of the credit card.
N = Does Not Match - The code entered is incorrect.

P = Is Not Processed - The code was not validated.
S = Should be on card but not so indicated - The customer left that field blank.
U = Issuer not certified or has not provided encryption key - The card issuing bank does not participate in the CVV2 program or
hasn't provided the key so that the code can be validated.

Why was my credit card transaction declined?
How to Review Transaction Details

Log into the Merchant Interface.
Click Transaction Search from the main toolbar.
Enter the relevant search criteria to find the transaction.
Click Search at the bottom of the page.
Click the appropriate Transaction ID to review the transaction details.

On the Transaction Detail page, the transaction status Declined is displayed directly under the Transaction ID. A brief reason for the
decline may be provided in parenthesis.
Common Decline Messages and Information
Here are some common decline messages and information about the decline types:
Declined (Card declined by issuer - contact card issuer to determine reason.)

The customer's credit card issuing bank did not approve the transaction. This could be due to insufficient funds, frozen account status,
invalid credit card number or expiration date, etc. The customer will need to contact the card issuing bank for more information. One
way to recognize a bank decline is to look at the transaction's Address Verification Service (AVS) status in the Authorization Information

Why was my credit card transaction declined?
How to Review Transaction Details
Log into the Merchant Interface.
Click Transaction Search from the main toolbar.
Enter the relevant search criteria to find the transaction.
Click Search at the bottom of the page.
Click the appropriate Transaction ID to review the transaction details.

On the Transaction Detail page, the transaction status Declined is displayed directly under the Transaction ID. A brief reason for the
decline may be provided in parenthesis.
Common Decline Messages and Information
Here are some common decline messages and information about the decline types:
Declined (Card declined by issuer - contact card issuer to determine reason.)

The customer's credit card issuing bank did not approve the transaction. This could be due to insufficient funds, frozen account status,
invalid credit card number or expiration date, etc. The customer will need to contact the card issuing bank for more information. One
way to recognize a bank decline is to look at the transaction's Address Verification Service (AVS) status in the Authorization Information

section of the Transaction Detail page. If it shows AVS Not Applicable (P), this generally means that no verification of the address
information was initiated as the transaction was declined beforehand.
Declined (Contact card issuer to complete transaction.)

Declined (Card reported lost or stolen - contact card issuer for resolution.)
The transaction status means that the credit card has been reported as lost or stolen. The credit card company is informing you, as the
merchant, that this card should be taken from the customer and destroyed. We understand this is impossible if the transaction was made

via the Internet. In any case, you may contact the customer to inform them of the card's status, and seek another method of payment.
Declined (AVS Mismatch) or (Card Code Mismatch)
This means the transaction was declined due to the Address Verification Service (AVS) or Card Code Verification (CCV) results. AVS

compares the billing address (numerical portions) provided in a transaction with the cardholder's address on file at the credit card issuing
bank. CCV compares the card code (a three- or four-digit security code that is printed on the credit card) provided in a transaction with

the card code on file at the credit card issuing bank. When enabled, both services return a code to the payment gateway indicating the
verification results. You can configure settings for AVS and CCV in the Merchant Interface to decline transactions based on those codes.
Declined (The transaction was declined as a result of triggering a Fraud Detection Suite filter.)

If the account has Fraud Detection Suite (FDS) or Advanced Fraud Detection Suite (AFDS) enabled, you can configure the payment
gateway to decline transactions that trigger specific FDS filters.

UPS Shipping with Bottlecapps
1. Create a UPS account at https://www.ups.com/doapp/signup?loc=en_US

returnto=https%3a//www.ups.com/upsdeveloperkit/requestaccesskey&reasonCode=-1 and
Sign In using the credentials from Step 1
Then, click on Step 4: Request an Access Key
3. Fill out the information in the new page
∙ Authenticate account information (using recent invoice or Pickup Postal Code on file with UPS) ∙

Once authentication is completed, you will be brought back to the Request Access Key page
∙ Click on the Request Access Key button at the bottom
4. The access key will now be displayed. The access key is 16 characters long and it is alpha-
numeric (Example: DD7B8AEWD19F1DE6)

5. Send the following fields to your Bottlecapps Rep:
- UPS Account Credentials (Username and Password from Step 1)
- Access key from Step 4 (Example: DD7B8AEWD19F1DE6)
- Account Number/Shipper Number (Example: A2569Y)
You can find your UPS account number, sometimes referred to as the shipper number, in various
ways.
3. By calling UPS at 1 (800) 742-5877.
4. By locating it on paper invoices or electronically for shippers signed up to UPS Billing
Center.
5. By finding the tracking number of a package that was shipped using your UPS account.
Look for the string of six numbers and letters that immediately follow “1Z”—that’s your
account number.

Document: Customer Segmentation | Category: SETUP > Marketing Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Customer Segmentation (Target Groups)
There are a number of reasons why segmenting customers into distinct groups is a useful endeavor for marketing
purposes. This allows a store to send Email Blasts, Push Notifications, or even SMS Messages (texts) straight to
the target group. Here’s how to do this:
First, click the SEGMENTATION tab on the menu in the webconsole (liquorapps.com).
Then, click ADD SEGMENTATION ...

In the demo example, we are creating a target group of scotch and bourbon drinkers. Select the corresponding
boxes ...
The next step is to select which stores this applies to and then click SAVE ...
Now that the list has been created, the store can see the details of the newly segmented group of customers. To see
HOW MANY are in each group, click the little eye under the Export column (circled in green below) and it will
show the total.

NOTE – the total number of customers that fit each profile changes all the time, so the store must click the eye
every single time to see the refreshed total.
Customers are added via three methods:
They have ordered the selected product(s) before
They have added the selected product(s) to their cart before
They have marked the selected product(s) as “favorites”
Now that the list is created, one way to reach these customers is via an email blast (here is a quick tutorial on how
to use the EMAIL BUILDER).
To do this, click on the EMAIL BUILDER tab on the left side and then click BLANK to start from scratch (or you
can use STAFF PICKS or whatever else applies) ...
The next screen asks if you would like to add products, so in this case, we can type in the search bar and add some
bourbons/whiskeys/scotches or whichever products the store would like to send to the segmented target group of
customers:

On the next page, first name the template something that makes sense ...
On the right, you can build everything the way you like with the side menu ...

These can be dragged to the body of the email builder ...
Note, if you want to start the email with the store logo at the top, enter [[STORELOGO]] in the Image URL box
circled below on the right and it will automatically be added ...

Continue making the email look the way you want. In this example, we chose to create a new column that was
three boxes wide. In each box, we dragged a PRODUCT over from the right, then selected the products we added
from the earlier step:
Once you are done, click SAVE & CONTINUE ...
On the next screen, fill out the boxes and then click the drop down menu to choose the target audience that we
created ...

After that, you can send it to your customers (or send a test to yourself to see how it turned out) ...
Push Notifications are very simple. Click on the NOTIFICATIONS tab on the left, then you will see a drop down
called TARGET GROUP ...
SMS (text messaging) is very similar. Click SMS MESSAGING on the left menu and then TARGET GROUP ...

Document: Email Builder Guide | Category: SETUP > Marketing Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Email Marketing Guide
EMAIL MARKETING GUIDE
One of the best ways to stay in constant communication with customers is via the Webconsole’s Email Builder
(liquorapps.com). This tool allows stores to craft tailored messages that go out to either all customers, or to even
more targeted groups of customers when using the SEGMENTATION feature. Here’s a quick guide on how to
use the EMAIL BUILDER:
On the left side menu, click Email Builder. From there, you will see pre-made templates at the very top, then
below that, any custom-made templates that have been made in the past, and below that, an easily browsable list
of recently used templates ...
Let’s say you pick one of the pre-made templates at the top, in this case, FLASH SALE ...

The first thing you will see when clicking FLASH SALE is a popup that will ask if you would like to add
products. If you want products in your email, this is the time to add them:
Once the template is open, you will see boxes in the main part of the email that need to be filled out. To do that,
drag the appropriate box from the right side to the spot in the email where you want it. In this guide, we will be
showing only how to add PRODUCTS and the STORE LOGO, but the same principles apply to the other boxes.
See below ...
To add the store logo, click the top box and it will open a new side menu. You can add some other images here,
too, but to add the store logo, type [[STOREIMAGE]] in the box titled ImageURL. See below ...

Then, to add products, for example, drag the box that says PRODUCT from the right menu to the body of the
email. At first, the products will be generic placeholders like the ones in the green box below. Click on them to
reveal a new side menu where you can add the products.

You can then choose the products you added in Step 1:
Since we only chose 3 products and there were 4 columns in this template, you can change the layout to match. To
do that, just click anywhere in that particular row and a new side menu will ask you what layout you want. See
below ...

Now that there are only 3 boxes for 3 products, the layout looks good ...

Now that there are only 3 boxes for 3 products, the layout looks good ...

In fact, it’s recommended that you only do 3 products per row to ensure the email will display properly for all
email recipients. That is why you will see this note at the top ...
At this point, you can SAVE AS DRAFT and come back to it later or SAVE AND CONTINUE to advance to the
next step.
If you click SAVE AND CONTINUE, the next step is to add the EMAIL SUBJECT, the SCHEDULED TIME
TO SEND (or send immediately), and the TARGET GROUP that will receive this email ...

If you created a target audience by using the SEGMENTATION tool, that group will show up in the dropdown
menu called TARGET GROUP.
For instance, if you wanted this email to go out ONLY to customers that previously purchased bourbon at some
point, you can select that group from the TARGET GROUP dropdown (or just send to everyone). Here is the
guide for how to setup CUSTOMER SEGMENTATION.
At this point, you can go ahead and send it to your desired audience or send a test email to yourself to see how it
turned out ...
Again, for this example, we used the pre-made template called FLASH SALES, but you can use any template you
want, or create your own from scratch by clicking BLANK ...

The best way to learn this awesome feature is to dive right in and try it out. There are many ways to use the Email
Builder, and if done consistently, there are very few methods more tried-and-true to engage your customers!

Document: Product Collections | Category: SETUP > Marketing Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Product Collections Guide
The Product Collections feature is like creating an album or a playlist of your favorite music to share with
friends. This guide will show you how to create a unique URL with a list of products of your choosing. The list
can be whatever you choose, whether that’s rare, allocated items, or another specific type of product-grouping.
First, click the “Product Collections” tab on the left of the Webconsole (liquorapps.com):
Click “ADD COLLECTIONS” and you will see this dropdown:

Then, you can NAME the collection ...
And then start searching for products to add ...
Once you are satisfied with your collection (you can always edit later), click SAVE:
At this point, you can now click the PRODUCT COLLECTIONS tab again you will see the ones you’ve created
with a corresponding URL (Website link) next to it:

Now that you have a URL that takes customers straight to this new collection of products, how you share it is up
to you! You can send it in an email, or share it on your social media pages, or create a QR code for it and print it
out on some marketing materials (there are many free QR code generator websites available, just Google it!).

Document: Sms Marketing | Category: SETUP > Marketing Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
SMS Marketing
SMS Marketing is a feature that allows stores to engage customers with personalized messages, special offers,
promotions and updates. In this tutorial, we will explain how to use this feature.
To receive text messages, customers must first Opt-In to receive such communications. There are 3 main ways
customers can do this when browsing a store’s website:
1) UPPER RIGHT:
2) LOWER RIGHT:
3) AT CHECKOUT:

In the app, customers can Opt-In on the left menu (as well as at Checkout):

When a customer chooses to Opt-In and adds their phone number, they will receive the following text which
requires the customer to reply “YES” to complete the Opt-In process ...
Here’s how to set SMS Messaging up. First, open your Webconsole (liquorapps.com) and click the tab that says
SMS MARKETING on the left-hand menu ...

From this page, you can choose:
Store Selection (if you have more than one location) – choose which of your stores’ customers you would like
to send the text to.
Target Group – To send to all customers that have Opted-In, click All Customers
To send to specific SEGMENTED groups of customers (only bourbon drinkers, for example), you can
create Segmented Groups. The groups you create will show up in the TARGET GROUP section. Here
is how to set up Customer Segmentation.

SMS Message (Text Message) - this is where you create the message. You can include links, but no images
or emojis. You can sent immediately or schedule for later. You can also send a TEST message to a phone
number or your choosing.
Below this section, you can see a list of any scheduled messages as well as browse the history of messages you
have previously sent.
To use this feature (as well as all new features), your app and website must be updated to the latest version. If you
have any questions or need further assistance, please email help@bottlecapps.com.

Staff Picks
1. From liquorapps.com, click "Staff Picks"

Document: Subscriptions | Category: SETUP > Marketing Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Subscriptions (aka - Wine Club)
SUBSCRIPTIONS (aka - Wine Club)
Bottlecapps' Subscriptions feature is akin to a Wine Club whereby customers can pay a subscription fee to have
products delivered, ready for pickup, or even shipped directly to them. However, it is not limited to wine only.
NOTE: This feature is available only to stores using STRIPE or WORLD PAY as their payment gateway.
The tab to set up Subscriptions is in the Webconsole (liquorapps.com) on the left side.
Once you click the Subscriptions tab, you can rename it however you see fit in the box at the top:

To start, click “Add Subscription” in the upper right:
On the next page, fill out all the fields with the details of the subscription:
Choose the category:

To start, click “Add Subscription” in the upper right:
On the next page, fill out all the fields with the details of the subscription:
Choose the category:

Let’s say you choose WINE. You can then either pick your own image to add (1920x1080px), or choose one from
the library that makes sense for the subscription. In this example, wine:
After that, enter the “Billing day of the Month.” This will be when all customers get billed, not the day they
signed up. In this case, customers will be billed on the 15th of every month:

For Fulfillment Options, there are three options (some may not apply to all stores); Pickup, Delivery, or Shipping.
Stores can pick one or multiple options and the customer will then be able to pick which one they want. These can
be applied to one or multiple locations, if applicable.
If Delivery and/or Shipping is selected, there will be an option to add Fees and Taxes:
Below that, the store can create custom zones (Delivery and/or Shipping) that exclusively apply to Subscriptions.

NOTE: If there are no zones added to map part, it will default to the store’s zones previously set up in the “My
Account” tab.
Once completed, click NEXT. This is where you set up the Subscription Tiers:
The store, for example, may choose to name this tier “Two White Wines” and then provide a description the next
box. After that, choose or upload an image.
Then, the store must select how the tier works by choosing the TERM and PRICE:

In the example above, you will see Quantity is TWO because this subscription is for TWO WHITE WINES.
TERM: Choose any or all of the options customers can select from for the length of their subscription.
PRICE: Choose the price of each one. So, if a customer chooses “Monthly,” they would receive their two
white wines every month and pay $30. If a customer chooses every 3 months, that would be 6 bottles, and so
on. The customer is essentially paying for 3 months in advance.
NOTE: It’s common to reduce the price for longer terms, so rather than paying $90 every 3 months,
the store may choose to lower the price to $80, for example. Same principle applies for even longer
terms.
GIFT AVAILABLE: by selecting “Yes,” this means a customer can choose to gift this subscription to
someone else and the person receiving the gift would not see the price.
Next, the store can choose the category, if they so choose, as long as the number of bottles does not exceed the

someone else and the person receiving the gift would not see the price.
Next, the store can choose the category, if they so choose, as long as the number of bottles does not exceed the
amount originally selected for that tier. In our example, we said TWO bottles of white wine, so only two can be
selected from this section:

If the subscription is set up in the way shown above, the customer will not know what bottles they will be getting.
It will be up to the store to choose. However, there is another option where the store can add products to a list and
the customer can choose two bottles from there:
Once everything is set up, click ADD TIER at the bottom. On the next page, the store must click SAVE to make
these subscriptions available:

After hitting the save button, customers will then be able to click the SUBSCRIPTIONS tab at the top of store
websites (or on the side menu of apps) and then select which club they want to be a part of:
From there, customers can then choose from the options that were setup by the store and click SUBSCRIBE:
Once the customer clicks SUBSCRIBE, it will navigate them to a different checkout screen than the normal one.
(note - no other products can be added to a subscription):

Document: Shipping Order Processing Tutorial | Category: SETUP > Order Management Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Shipping Order Processing Tutorial
When a shipping order comes in, the store first checks if the product(s) is in stock (or finds out when they will
have it in stock). Then, they must go to the ORDERS tab and click “Accept Order,” then fill out the boxes and
click “Accept Order & Save ETA” ...
Once the products are actually ready to ship (NOT BEFORE OR IT WILL CAUSE PROBLEMS), the store clicks
“Preparing Shipment” ...
If the store has SHIPSTATION, that’s all that needs to be done. If the store has UPS (or in some less frequent
cases, WESHIP), this screen will popup ...

If the order is too large to fit in one box and instead needs two boxes, the store may need to print TWO labels, one
for each box. It will look like this ...
The store needs to put the number of bottles and the weight in these boxes. Then click “SAVE.” The order status
will then be updated to “Preparing Shipment” like the green circle below. The store then needs to open the order

by clicking the blue order number on the left (see green arrow) ...
Inside the order, the store will now see a Tracking Number, like this ...
The next step is to click the red “Shipping Label” button at the very bottom. This will create a popup that looks
like the one below with the arrow pointed to it. Then click “Shipping Label” in that popup (if there had been two
boxes selected from the earlier step, this popup would print TWO labels rather than one like you see below ...
The store will

then see this popup where they will click the Printer icon circled in green ...
That will bring up this popup. Click “Print” at the bottom and THAT IS THE LAST STEP. Once the store clicks
PRINT, the current status will be AUTOMATICALLY updated to SHIPPED. The store does NOT need to update
to Shipped manually.

Document: Updating Order Status And Customer Notifications | Category: SETUP > Order Management Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Updating Order Status And Customer Notifications
1. Click "orders" from liquorapps.com.
2. Click on the order you would like to update.
3. Click edit next to the pickup/delivery time if you need to edit the pickup or delivery time.

4. Select the date and time that it needs to be changed to and click update when finished.
5. Click here: to update the order status.
Please note the order status types will change based off of the order type (delivery, pickup, curbside, or shipping).
Once you update the order, the customer will get a push notification, text message and email letting them know the order has
been updated.

Document: How To Edit Curbside Settings | Category: SETUP > Setup Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
Curbside Pickup Guide
Curbside Pickup is a great tool for customers who want the convenience of ordering ahead of time and having
their order ready to pick up, but without having to go inside and wait in line. Staff will simply bring the order
right out to the customers' car.
Once an order is marked Ready for Pickup, the customer will automatically receive both an email notification
and a push notification. When they open the app or website, they will be prompted to check in for their pickup.
They will be asked to provide helpful details such as vehicle color, parking spot, and any other notes they would
like to share. This information instantly appears in the Webconsole (liquorapps.com) so store staff knows the
customer has arrived and can bring out the order.
1. Navigate to https://liquorapps.com
2. Click "My Account"
3. Click "Yes" on “Enable Curbside?” on the My Account Tab

4. Click the "Curbside" Tab
5. Update Minimum Order Amount for Curbside

6. Update Maximum Order Amount for Curbside.
7. Update Curbside Convenience Fee
8. “Is Enable Curbside Slots” will give you the ability to limit the number of orders you receive in a certain time
slot

Document: Seo Tags | Category: SETUP > Setup Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
SEO Tags Guide
Maintain SEO Tags
SEO or Search Engine Optimization tags are attributes used to provide search engines with information about a
webpage's content, helping them understand and rank the page in search results. Key SEO tags include page
titles, page descriptions, and header tags, all of which play a vital role in improving a website's visibility and
click-through rate.
If you have signed up for our SEO Pro package, these settings are maintained for you by our SEO team and no
action is needed by you.
To create a SEO tag for a specific webpage, click on the “Add SEO Tag” button
On the following page, add the below attributes.
URL: This is the website URL of the page for which you are adding the tags for. E.g.
demo.bottlecapps.com/aboutus
Title: This is the title of the page that is shown in search engine results.
Description: This is description about the webpage and a good place to include some keywords that are displayed
on the page
Below is how the title and description display in a google search.

Keywords: Use this field to specify the keywords you would like to target for search. Use comma to enter
multiple keywords. For best results, the keywords you enter here should also exist as content on your page.
H1 to H4 Tags: H1, H2, H3 & H4 tags are used to define different heading of the webpage. H1 tag defines the
main heading of the webpage and is the most important one. Other tags are optional. Incorporate the primary
keyword for the page within H1 tag.
Save your changes once you are done entering all the fields.
Document: User Management Guide | Category: SETUP > Setup Guides

BOTTLECAPPS
User Management Guide
Bottlecapps’ User Management feature allows stores the ability to control and/or limit Webconsole access to
individual store employees (i.e. - managers, general staff, drivers, outside marketing companies, etc.).
For example, delivery drivers for a store may need only the ability to process orders, not send Push Notifications
or set up Flash Sales. Up until now, the BC Admin app was the only way to restrict such usage for drivers.
On the other hand, a store may want a marketing representative to be able to login and create banners, send email
blasts and Push Notifications, and other marketing-related tasks. Once again, this tool allows restricted access for
just these things. This guide will cover all the ways this tool can help stores manage user roles.
To get to the User Management tool, start by clicking on MY ACCOUNT on the left side menu of the
Webconsole (liquorapps.com):
Then click "Admin User Management" ...

Then click “Add Admin Users” ...
NOTE: Only ADMIN LEVEL users can add more users via this tool, but importantly, all users that have been
granted access the store’s Webconsole in the past will be considered “Admin Level” by default until changed by
the store.
After clicking “Add Admin Users,” the next screen will provide the opportunity to add user-roles and dictate what
they can or cannot see or control:

The Roles are as follows:
STORE ADMIN – this role is the highest level of access. No restrictions and the user can add other users.
ORDER – this role limits users access exclusively to the Orders tab and the BC Admin App.
MARKETING – this role restricts users to Push Notifications, Segmentation, SMS Marketing, Email
Builder, Product Collections, and App/Website Banners.
NOTE: this does not grant access to the Coupons tab at this time as anything that affects pricing is
limited to Store Admin.
SEO – this role limits users to the SEO Tags tab only.
NOTE: A Store Admin can grant a single user multiple roles.
Once the users info has been added and the roles have been selected, click ADD and the user will receive this
email asking them to accept the invitation. The link inside the email expires in 24 hours:

They will then need to set up a password and login to complete the process.
Once the user logs in to the store’s Webconsole, as previously outlined, they will be restricted to the particular
roles the Store Admin has assigned. Clicking on any tab outside of said role will prompt this screen:
To see who all has what roles currently and make edits/adjustments or resend the invitation, click "Admin User
Management" in the MY ACCOUNT tab ...

One final note for chain stores, assigning roles must be done ONE STORE LOCATION at a time. However,
further enhancements are in the works that will allow a user to be granted roles for multiple locations all from one
screen at one time.
